creator: yuri@freebsd.org
text:
Hi Wen,


It happened such that the broken py39-threadpoolctl-3.1.0 is currently in the ports tree.

IMO you should just add it as dependency.

This statements currently fails:
> from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
>   File "/disk-samsung/freebsd-ports/devel/py-jsonpickle/x.py", line 1, in <module>
>     from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sklearn/__init__.py", line 82, in <module>
>     from .base import clone
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sklearn/base.py", line 17, in <module>
>     from .utils import _IS_32BIT
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sklearn/utils/__init__.py", line 29, in <module>
>     from .fixes import parse_version, threadpool_info
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sklearn/utils/fixes.py", line 20, in <module>
>     import threadpoolctl
> ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'threadpoolctl'

But it succeeds when threadpoolctl is installed.
